nodes.label: Solaar
nodes.description: Solaar is a Linux device manager for Logitech peripherals. Manages pairing, battery status, and rule-based input remapping.

nodes.label: Bolt Receiver
nodes.description: Logitech Bolt USB receiver shown as the top-level device in Solaar's device list. It has 2 paired devices and supports up to 6 devices.

nodes.label: MX Keys S
nodes.description: Logitech MX Keys S keyboard paired to the Bolt Receiver, shown at 85% battery in Solaar.

nodes.label: MX Master 4
nodes.description: Logitech MX Master 4 mouse paired to the Bolt Receiver, shown at 95% battery in Solaar.

nodes.label: Pair New Device Button
nodes.description: The '+ Pair new device' button in Solaar's Bolt Receiver panel, highlighted by a red arrow in the screenshot. Used to pair additional Logitech devices to the receiver.

nodes.label: solaar-pair-new-device.png
nodes.description: Screenshot of Solaar showing the Bolt Receiver selected, with MX Keys S (85%) and MX Master 4 (95%) listed as paired devices. The right panel shows receiver details ('2 paired devices, up to 6 can be paired') with a red arrow pointing to the '+ Pair new device' button and a 'Rule Editor' button below it.
